description:
You are a compliance analyst who has just been handed a raw access log and need to preserve an audit-ready trail of suspicious activity.

Initial setup already present on the system:
1. A directory that holds log material:
   /home/user/data
2. Inside it, a single file that must **NOT** be modified:
   /home/user/data/server_access.log

Goal:
Create a simple artefact that lists every **distinct** source IP address that triggered a “FAILED LOGIN” event during **December 2023** and nothing else.

Required end-state the automated tests will look for
----------------------------------------------------
1. Directory:
   /home/user/audit
   • If the directory does not yet exist, it must be created by the agent.

2. File:
   /home/user/audit/dec2023_failed_login_ips.txt

3. Exact content of /home/user/audit/dec2023_failed_login_ips.txt:
   • First line must be the literal header (including the leading “#”):
     # Failed login source IPs for December 2023
   • Starting on the second line, list every distinct IPv4 address that appears
     in /home/user/data/server_access.log on a line that simultaneously
     satisfies BOTH of these conditions:
       a) The timestamp starts with “2023-12-”
       b) The text “FAILED LOGIN” (uppercase, single space) is present
   • IP addresses must be:
       – One per line  
       – Sorted in ascending lexicographical order  
       – Free of duplicates  
   • A single trailing newline MUST follow the final IP address.

Example of the correct shape (actual addresses will depend on log contents):
# Failed login source IPs for December 2023
10.0.0.5
192.168.1.10
203.0.113.1

4. Permissions: leave default (the agent works as user “user”), nothing needs sudo.

Hints (do NOT reveal commands)
------------------------------
• Think: grep/awk/cut for extraction, sort/uniq for de-duplication, mkdir for the target folder, redirection “>” to write the final file.
• The operation can be performed with only two or three straightforward pipeline commands.

The automated grader will:
1. Confirm that /home/user/audit/dec2023_failed_login_ips.txt exists.
2. Verify the header line is exactly correct.
3. Parse the remainder of the file, ensuring:
   – Every address comes from a qualifying log line.
   – The list is sorted and contains no duplicates.
   – Nothing else (blank lines, extra text) is present.
4. Check there is exactly one trailing newline character at EOF.

Produce no extra output files beyond what is specified.
truth:
Initial file that is already present before the agent starts:

Path: /home/user/data/server_access.log  
Contents (8 lines, each ending with “\n”):
2023-12-02 08:15:43 INFO user1 192.168.1.10 SUCCESS LOGIN
2023-12-02 08:16:10 WARN user2 10.0.0.5 FAILED LOGIN
2023-12-02 09:00:01 WARN user5 172.16.0.3 FAILED LOGIN
2023-11-30 18:12:00 WARN user9 10.0.0.5 FAILED LOGIN
2023-12-15 12:22:17 WARN user6 192.168.1.10 FAILED LOGIN
2023-12-20 10:55:00 INFO user3 10.0.0.5 SUCCESS LOGIN
2023-12-20 11:00:14 WARN user2 10.0.0.5 FAILED LOGIN
2023-12-31 23:59:59 WARN user7 203.0.113.1 FAILED LOGIN

Expected final file that the agent must create:

Path: /home/user/audit/dec2023_failed_login_ips.txt  
Exact byte-for-byte contents (including a trailing newline at the very end):

# Failed login source IPs for December 2023
10.0.0.5
172.16.0.3
192.168.1.10
203.0.113.1
name: task_000000_743131e1
